## FHIR Queries: NAFLD Patient Population Identification

---

### 🏥 Group 1 — NAFLD Diagnoses (Condition Resource)

**1A — ICD-10-CM Coded NAFLD / MASLD / MASH / NASH / Hepatic Steatosis**
> Covers: K76.0 (Fatty liver NEC / NAFLD / MASLD / hepatic steatosis) · K75.81 (NASH / MASH / nonalcoholic steatohepatitis)

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K76.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K75.81&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&_include=Condition:subject
```

**1B — SNOMED CT Coded NAFLD / NASH**
> Covers: 197315008 (Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease) · 735373009 (Nonalcoholic steatohepatitis)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|197315008,http://snomed.info/sct|735373009&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&_include=Condition:subject
```

**1C — NAFLD-Related Cirrhosis (ICD-10-CM)**
> Covers: K74.60 (Unspecified cirrhosis) · K74.69 (Other cirrhosis) — *requires clinical review to confirm NAFLD etiology*

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K74.60,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K74.69&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 🧪 Group 2 — Elevated Liver Enzymes (Observation Resource)

**2A — Elevated ALT > 40 U/L**
> LOINC: 1742-6 (ALT general) · 1743-4 (ALT without P-5'-P) · 1744-2 (ALT with P-5'-P) · 76625-3 (ALT in whole blood)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1742-6,http://loinc.org|1743-4,http://loinc.org|1744-2,http://loinc.org|76625-3&value-quantity=gt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|U/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

**2B — Elevated AST > 40 U/L**
> LOINC: 1920-8 (AST general) · 1916-6 (AST without P-5'-P) · 30239-8 (AST with P-5'-P)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1920-8,http://loinc.org|1916-6,http://loinc.org|30239-8&value-quantity=gt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|U/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### 📋 Query Summary & Union Logic

| Query | Resource | Criteria | Specificity |
|-------|----------|----------|-------------|
| 1A | Condition | ICD-10 K76.0, K75.81 | ⭐⭐⭐ High |
| 1B | Condition | SNOMED 197315008, 735373009 | ⭐⭐⭐ High |
| 1C | Condition | ICD-10 K74.60, K74.69 | ⭐⭐ Medium (needs review) |
| 2A | Observation | ALT > 40 U/L | ⭐ Low (broad net) |
| 2B | Observation | AST > 40 U/L | ⭐ Low (broad net) |

---

### ⚠️ Important Clinical Caveats

1. **Exclusion criteria needed** — Groups 2A/2B cast a very wide net. Patients with competing diagnoses should be excluded post-query:
   - **Alcoholic liver disease** — `K70.x`
   - **Viral hepatitis** — `B18.x`, `B19.x`
   - **Autoimmune hepatitis** — `K75.4`
   - **Drug-induced liver injury** — `K71.x`

2. **Enzyme thresholds** — Some guidelines use sex-specific cutoffs (ALT ≥30 for women). Consider running a second pass with `gt29` if capturing borderline cases.

3. **Recency of labs** — Add `date=ge2024-01-01` to Observation queries to focus on recent results rather than historical spikes.

4. **MASLD naming** — As of 2023, MASLD/MASH replaced NAFLD/NASH terminology. ICD-10-CM K76.0 / K75.81 remain the current billing codes; some EHRs may lag in adopting SNOMED updates.

5. **`_include`** — All queries include `_include` to co-return Patient resources so you can deduplicate by patient ID across result sets for your union population.

**To build the union patient list:**

2. De-duplicate across all 5 queries using the Patient resource `id`

### 🔴 Hepatic Fibrosis & End-Stage Cirrhosis (ICD-10-CM: K74.x)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K74.00,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K74.01,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K74.02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K74.60,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K74.69&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 🔵 Full Spectrum — SNOMED CT (All Stages)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|76783007,http://snomed.info/sct|197315008,http://snomed.info/sct|197321007,http://snomed.info/sct|57773001,http://snomed.info/sct|19943007&_include=Condition:subject
```

## 📋 Code Reference Table

| Stage | Description | ICD-10-CM | SNOMED CT |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Simple steatosis / NAFLD / MASLD** | Fatty liver without inflammation | `K76.0` | `76783007`, `197315008` |
| **NASH / MASH** | Steatohepatitis (metabolic-associated) | `K75.81` | `197321007` |
| **Generic steatohepatitis** | Inflammation + fat, unspecified etiology | `K75.81` | `57773001` |
| **Hepatic fibrosis** (early→advanced) | Progressive liver scarring | `K74.00`, `K74.01`, `K74.02` | — |
| **Cirrhosis** | End-stage fibrosis | `K74.60`, `K74.69` | `19943007` |

> **Notes:**
> - `K74.69` ("Other cirrhosis") captures **cryptogenic cirrhosis**, which frequently represents burned-out NASH
> - MASLD (2023 consensus renaming) currently maps to `K76.0` (steatosis) or `K75.81` (MASH) in ICD-10-CM — no separate code yet

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Elevated ALT or AST (> 40 IU/L)

Two separate Observation queries are needed — one per analyte — with unit variants to account for how IU/L may be encoded differently across systems (`[IU]/L` vs `U/L` in UCUM):

```
// ALT > 40 IU/L (LOINC 1742-6 — Serum/Plasma, most common)
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1742-6&value-quantity=gt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|[IU]/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject

// ALT > 40 U/L (alternate UCUM encoding)
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1742-6&value-quantity=gt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|U/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject

// AST > 40 IU/L (LOINC 1920-8 — Serum/Plasma, most common)
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1920-8&value-quantity=gt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|[IU]/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject

// AST > 40 U/L (alternate UCUM encoding)
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1920-8&value-quantity=gt40|http://unitsofmeasure.org|U/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Key Design Decisions

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| **ALT LOINC** | `1742-6` | Alanine aminotransferase, Serum/Plasma (primary clinical code) |
| **AST LOINC** | `1920-8` | Aspartate aminotransferase, Serum/Plasma (primary clinical code) |
| **Threshold** | `gt40` | Standard ULN; > 40 triggers liver injury workup |
| **Units (dual)** | `[IU]/L` and `U/L` | Both UCUM codes are used interchangeably in production systems |
| **`status=final`** | Filters out prelim/amended | Avoids acting on unreliable results |
| **`_include=Observation:subject`** | Returns linked Patient | Enables direct patient identification in one call |

### Additional LOINC Codes to Consider
- **ALT whole blood**: `76625-3`
- **AST whole blood**: `30239-8`
- **ALT w/ P-5'-P**: `1743-4` / without: `1744-2`
